state_addenda_present.focus: California Fast Food Act (AB 1228, Cal. Lab. Code §§ 1474-1475) disclosures
state_addenda_present.summary: Franchisee must independently determine if Fast Food Act applies and comply (minimum wages, hours, working conditions). McDonald's does not provide training or assistance related to Fast Food Act compliance and charges no Fast Food Act-specific fees. The Item 7 Additional Funds estimate includes increased costs (e.g., higher wages) to the extent the Act is in effect as of the FDD issuance date.

No acknowledgement or questionnaire signed in connection with commencement of the franchise relationship can waive claims under applicable state franchise law (including fraud in the inducement) or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements. This provision supersedes conflicting contract terms. Standard Hawaii Franchise Investment Law filing notice also included.

state_addenda_present.focus: Item 17 — renewal, transfer, cause, forum, law, limitations period
state_addenda_present.summary: Any release required as a condition of renewal/assignment/transfer does NOT apply to liability under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law. Termination on insolvency may be unenforceable under federal bankruptcy law. Franchisee may bring MD-law claims in Maryland subject to arbitration and FAA preemption. MD-law claims must be brought within 3 years after grant of the franchise.

state_addenda_present.focus: Items 13 and 17 — trademark protection, termination notice, consent to transfer
state_addenda_present.summary: Item 13: McDonald's will protect franchisees against trademark infringement/unfair competition claims arising from authorized use. Item 17: Nothing in the FDD or FA shall abrogate Minnesota Statutes Chapter 80C rights. McDonald's will comply with Minn. Stat. Sec. 80C.14 Subds. 3, 4, 5: 90 days' notice of termination with 60 days to cure; 180 days' notice for non-renewal; consent to transfer shall not be unreasonably withheld.

Non-compete covenants are generally unenforceable under North Dakota law, but McDonald's will seek to enforce to the extent enforceable. North Dakota law governs claims arising under the North Dakota Franchise Investment Law.

state_addenda_present.focus: Item 17 — relationship-law supremacy + Assurance of Discontinuance
state_addenda_present.summary: Inconsistent FA/FDD provisions are preempted by RCW 19.100.180 / Washington Franchise Investment Protection Act where the Act applies. Separate Washington Assurance of Discontinuance (AOD): McDonald's agreed to remove no-hire/no-poach provisions from form FA, not enforce existing ones, ask Washington franchisees to amend existing agreements, and notify franchisees. No monetary penalty. Issued in connection with Washington AG investigation alleging antitrust and unfair-practices violations.

wisconsin_finding: No Wisconsin-specific addendum is included in Exhibit T, despite WI being the state of filing (638437-2025 WI DFI). The default Illinois venue + Illinois governing law + 18-month/10-mile post-term non-compete apply in full to Wisconsin franchisees under this FDD.
other_material_findings: Only 6 states have any Exhibit T addendum. Other regulated states (IL, NY, VA, MI, RI, IN, SD, WI) rely on front-matter Michigan notice (pp.5–6) and the 'Special Risks' page, not on state-specific post-text addenda.
Washington AOD is the only state-level regulatory enforcement action currently live on the form agreement.
